description:
Advances: specled.spec_review

The Decisions Changed section (lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex:1270-1344) is purely descriptive: "here are the ADRs that changed." But ADR governance is a first-class concept in docs/concepts.md — append_only/* findings (requirement_deletion_without_adr, modal_downgrade_without_adr, scenario_regression_without_adr, adr_deletion, same_pr_self_authorization, etc.) need a place to live.

Render append_only/* findings inline in the Decisions panel, next to the ADR they should/should-not have authorized. When a requirement is deleted and no ADR with change_type ∈ {deprecates, weakens, narrows-scope, adds-exception} authorizes it, show "this PR deletes requirement R but no ADR authorizes the weakening" with a fix: hint reading the same code-fenced fix block the finding emits.

Depends on child #1 (governance leg in the classifier).

Files touched:
- lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex
- test/specled_ex/review/html_test.exs
- .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md
- .spec/state.json

Tests added: 6 new tests under "render_decisions_changed — append_only governance findings" in test/specled_ex/review/html_test.exs covering: empty panel still renders; orphan governance subsection appears for unauthorized requirement deletion; the code-fenced fix block is preserved verbatim; entity_id matching renders inline under the ADR card; mixed inline+orphan placement; non-append_only findings ignored.

Verification stubs flipped: existing mix test test/specled_ex/review/html_test.exs stub now also covers specled.spec_review.decisions_governance_inline.

Spec change: added requirement specled.spec_review.decisions_governance_inline to .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md and wired it into the html.ex source_file + html_test command verification stubs.

Summary: When a PR weakens a spec without an authorizing ADR, the Decisions Changed section of mix spec.review now surfaces the append_only/* finding directly in that panel — inline next to the ADR it should have authorized when entity_id resolves, otherwise in a "Governance violations" subsection at the top of the panel. The original code-fenced `fix:` block from SpecLedEx.AppendOnly.analyze is rendered verbatim in a <pre> so the remediation contract is the same as the CLI surfaces.

Verification: mix test (2 properties, 609 tests, 0 failures); mix spec.check (status=pass errors=0 warnings=0).

title: Decisions panel surfaces append_only governance violations
